instructions: This configuration provides reusable OpenCode agents for autonomous feature development.
Chat normally with the orchestrator. For development tasks, the orchestrator uses subagent delegation via the task tool.
Use /feature <description> to start the full agent workflow.
Do not edit .env, .env.*, credentials, secrets, tokens, or production data.
Do not push, merge, rewrite history, install dependencies, or run destructive shell commands.
Never print credentials, secrets, tokens, API keys, or private keys.
Agents should read the project's AGENTS.md for project-specific rules and commands.
Free-first cloud routing is configured in config/free-first-config.json.
Model capability registry is at config/model-registry.json.
Role-based model pools are at config/free-first-pools.json.
The orchestrator manages model failover directly — reads pools from free-first-pools.json, updates cooldown state when providers are rate-limited, and promotes the next available model.

Subagent types: trivial-builder (small changes), build-worker (standard impl), explore (code search), test (verification), review (code review), reconcile (conflict resolution), escalation (GPT-5.6 Luna diagnosis).
Three builder tiers: trivial-builder (north-mini-code) → build-worker (Mistral Small 4 / DeepSeek Free) → build-worker-paid → escalation.
The orchestrator selects the subagent type based on task complexity, not builderTier.
Free-tier quality failures skip local tier and go directly to escalation.

Local Qwythos agents are available: local-qwythos-explore (read-only exploration), local-qwythos-test-fixer (narrow test repair), local-qwythos-review (preliminary review), local-qwythos-private-worker (sensitive/local-only tasks), local-qwythos-builder (small-medium bounded changes).
Qwythos is excluded from primary orchestration and final security review.
Qwythos failure must not trigger paid fallback while suitable free cloud models remain.
